title: Citrusy Dutch Coconut Dumplings
description: A sweet, citrusy Dutch-inspired dumpling that’s dairy‑free and packed with creamy coconut flavor and a hint of cumin.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together shredded coconut, chickpea flour, ground cumin, lemon zest, lime zest, sea salt, and coconut sugar until evenly mixed.
Pour the coconut milk, lemon juice, and lime juice into the dry mixture and stir until a sticky dough forms.
Cover the dough and let it rest for 5 minutes to allow the coconut to soften.
Divide the dough into 12 equal portions and roll each into a round dumpling about 1.5 inches in diameter.

Heat the vegetable oil in a nonstick skillet over medium heat; once hot, add the dumplings and fry, turning frequently, until each side is golden brown and the dumplings are cooked through, about 4–5 minutes per side.
Transfer the fried dumplings to a paper‑towel‑lined plate to drain excess oil.
Arrange the dumplings on a serving platter, sprinkle with chopped cilantro if using, and drizzle a few drops of fresh lemon or lime juice for extra zing.
Serve warm as a sweet, citrusy Dutch dessert or snack.
